answer:
The correct answer is G. Thirst is primarily stimulated by increased plasma osmolarity (concentrated blood) and decreased extracellular fluid volume, both of which signal dehydration. Low blood sodium and low plasma osmolarity would actually inhibit thirst, while high extracellular fluid volume would also not trigger it. Thus, the combination of low extracellular fluid volume and high plasma osmolarity directly drives the sensation of thirst.

\boxed{G}
